samples.xml_description: Implement an unsigned 8bit multiplier based on pipelining processing. Clock. Active-low reset. Input enable bit for multiplication. Input multiplicand. Input multiplier. Output enable bit for multiplication. Output product. MUL_WIDTH is the input/output width. Reset all registers. if (!rst_n) begin mul_en_out_reg <= 0; mul_a_reg <= 0; mul_b_reg <= 0; mul_out_reg <= 0; mul_en_out <= 0; for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin sum[i] <= 0; carry[i] <= 0; end for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH*2-1; i = i + 1) begin temp[i] <= 0; end end Sample mul_en_in at the positive edge of the clock and store it in mul_en_out_reg. mul_en_out_reg <= mul_en_in; Sample input at the positive edge of the clock and store it in mul_a_reg and mul_b_reg. if (mul_en_in) begin mul_a_reg <= mul_a; mul_b_reg <= mul_b; end Generate partial products based on the bits of mul_b_reg. for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH; i = i + 1) begin if (mul_b_reg[i] == 1'b1) begin temp[i + MUL_WIDTH - 1 : i] <= mul_a_reg; end else begin temp[i + MUL_WIDTH - 1 : i] <= {MUL_WIDTH{1'b0}}; end end Calculate partial sums based on the generated partial products. for (int i = 0; i < MUL_WIDTH - 1; i = i + 1) begin if (temp[i + MUL_WIDTH - 1 : i] && sum[i]) begin sum[i + 1] <= ~sum[i]; carry[i] <= 1; end else begin sum[i + 1] <= sum[i]; carry[i] <= 0; end end Calculate the final product by adding the last two partial sums. if (carry[MUL_WIDTH - 2]) begin mul_out_reg <= sum[MUL_WIDTH - 2] + 1; end else begin mul_out_reg <= sum[MUL_WIDTH - 2]; end Assign the output mul_out based on the mul_en_out signal. if (mul_en_out) begin mul_out <= mul_out_reg; end else begin mul_out <= 0; end
